critical_success: Skol! The Norns themselves applaud... this once.
By Odin's missing eye, that was almost worthy of a saga.
Even I must admit, that was not terrible. Write it down.
The Valkyries pause mid-flight. Impressive, mortal.
A blow fit for Tyr's own sword arm. Begrudging respect.
Hmph. Even a blind squirrel finds an acorn in Yggdrasil.
Thor himself would nod. Once. Grudgingly.
The skalds will sing of this. Briefly.
I have witnessed ten thousand rolls. That one I will remember.
Well struck. Do not let it go to your head, it is still attached.
success: Adequate. The ravens will not mock you today.
You cleared the bar. The bar was on the ground, but still.
A passing grade in the school of not dying. Congratulations.
The dice show mercy. Enjoy it while it lasts.
Competent. A word rarely applied to your kind.
The Norns weave in your favor. For now.
Not bad. Not good, mind you, but not bad.
You survive another moment. How novel.
The thread holds. Barely, but it holds.
Acceptable. I have seen worse. I have seen much worse.
failure: Uff da, that was poor. Even by your standards.
The Norns wince. They expected nothing and are still disappointed.
I have seen better from a sleeping goat.
Perhaps adventuring is not your calling. Have you considered farming?
The dice speak, and they say 'nei.'
A swing and a miss worthy of Loki's worst jest.
Odin gave his eye for wisdom. You seem to have given yours for free.
The saga of your incompetence grows another verse.
Even the Midgard Serpent would pity that attempt.
Somewhere, a frost giant laughs. At you, specifically.
fumble: By the nine realms, what was that?
I... I am speechless. And I have been talking for a thousand years.
The Valkyries have removed you from their shortlist. Permanently.
Even Ragnarok would be embarrassed by that performance.
You have rolled with the grace of a one-legged troll on ice.
Loki himself could not have planned a more spectacular failure.
The All-Father weeps. Not for you. For the dice you have dishonored.
I will record this in the annals under 'Cautionary Tales.'
Uff da. That is the sound of your ancestors sighing in Valhalla.
Truly, the worst roll since Baldur forgot to check for mistletoe.
combat_start: Steel rings on steel. The saga demands blood.
They come. Let the Norns sort the worthy from the worm-food.
Blades out, mortal. The Burnwillow hungers.
Another fight. Another chance to disappoint me. Begin.
The enemy draws near. Try not to trip over your own sword.
To arms! Or to legs, whichever you manage first.
I smell violence. Finally, something worth narrating.
The shadows stir. Combat is upon you, ready or not.
combat_win: They fall. The skalds add another verse. A short one.
Victory. Do not grow accustomed to the feeling.
The enemy is slain. Loot quickly, before the next wave.
You survive. I will try to sound more surprised next time.
Well fought. The Valkyries return to their mead, disappointed.
The battle ends. Your ancestors nod. Slightly.
Room cleared. The Burnwillow grants a moment of silence. Use it wisely.
Another foe vanquished. The doom clock, however, is unimpressed.
general: Another day, another dungeon. The skalds grow weary of the routine.
Onwards, then. The saga writes itself, whether you are ready or not.
The crossroads await your next decision. Choose wisely. Or do not. I will narrate either way.
The wind shifts. Something stirs in the deep places.
Fortune favors the bold. Unfortunately, it also favors the stupid.
I have seen this before. It rarely ends well.
The threads of fate tangle. As usual, you are the knot.
Steel yourself, adventurer. The worst is always yet to come.
The Burnwillow remembers all who enter. It forgets none who fall.
Ja, ja, keep moving. The doom clock does not pause for the indecisive.
rot_hunter_near: Something scrapes in the dark. It draws closer. The Rot Hunter hunts.
Do you hear that? Claws on stone. It has your scent, mortal.
The air grows thick with blight. The Hunter is near. Very near.
Run or fight, but do not stand still. The Rot Hunter does not pause.
By the Norns, the Hunter closes in. Pray your blade is sharper than its hunger.
